title: archineottia pantlingii
side_effects: Archineottia pantlingii can have side effects if used improperly, such as causing severe allergic reactions, including hives, itching, and difficulty breathing in some individuals. Ingesting the plant's toxic compounds can lead to gastrointestinal issues, including nausea, vomiting, and abdominal pain. Prolonged exposure to the plant's sap or extracts may cause dermatitis, characterized by redness, inflammation, and blistering of the skin. In rare cases, improper use of Archineottia pantlingii may trigger more serious health complications, such as kidney or liver damage, requiring immediate medical attention.
